title: Simple beam bending and deflection
description_for_llm: Linear elastic (Theory I) analysis of one or more straight beams under a point load plus self weight. Use for single span or cantilever line elements where bending and deflection dominate. Assumptions: beam axes are straight curves in metres, supports at curve ends, one steel cross section for all members, loads are static and vertical. Limits: no buckling check, no dynamic or wind load, no material nonlinearity, curved members are not handled. NOT YET AUDITED: the shipped GHX carries its own internal sliders and has no ALMOND_IN_*/ALMOND_OUT_* harness params, so run_definition will refuse it. Until harnessed it is retrieval context only, useful for reading how a Karamba beam model is assembled.

inputs.name: ALMOND_IN_LINES
inputs.description: Beam axis curves, node-merged at shared endpoints.

inputs.name: ALMOND_IN_LOAD_KN
inputs.description: Downward point load applied at midspan or the free end.

outputs.name: ALMOND_OUT_DISP_MM
outputs.description: Largest nodal displacement in the model.

outputs.name: ALMOND_OUT_UTIL
outputs.description: Utilization ratio per element, 1.0 means at capacity.

outputs.name: ALMOND_OUT_MASS_KG
outputs.description: Total structural mass of the model.
